issues.detail: The ground truth data sources are limited to the project's own synthetic testbed and a single prior work (FP-02). There is no mention of using any external, independent datasets to validate the findings.
issues.suggestion: Consider including some real-world multi-agent incident reports or other external datasets to supplement the synthetic ground truth, even if the data is limited. This would help strengthen the external validity of the results.

issues.detail: The 'defense-aware' attacker is able to recover a significant portion (54%) of the advantage provided by the zero-trust defense. This suggests the zero-trust defense may not be as robust as claimed against a sophisticated adversary.
issues.suggestion: Expand the adaptive adversary evaluation to include a wider range of attack strategies and techniques. Quantify the robustness of the zero-trust defense against different levels of adversarial knowledge and capability.

issues.detail: The statistical plan includes appropriate non-parametric tests (bootstrap CI) and multiple comparison correction (Bonferroni). However, the effect size threshold of ≥10pp cascade rate difference between trust models is not strongly justified.
issues.suggestion: Consider providing a more detailed rationale for the 10pp effect size threshold, such as referencing prior work, industry standards, or practical significance for the target application domain.

issues.detail: The related work checklist is truncated in the provided text, suggesting the full list may not be available.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the full related work checklist is included in the final design document to demonstrate a comprehensive review of the relevant literature.
